questions.question: What will be the Simple Interest on a principal amount of Rs. 9800 at an interest rate of 6% per annum over 5 years?
What is the average of the following 5 consecutive even numbers: 25, 27, 29, 31, 33?
questions.trick: For a consecutive arithmetic sequence, the average is simply the exact middle term.

questions.question: A can complete a piece of work in 30 days while B can complete the same work in 46 days. If they work together, in how many days will they complete the work?
questions.explanation: Work = 30 * 46 = 1380. Rate = 30 + 46 = 76. Time = Work / Rate = 18.16 days.

questions.question: Rs. 462 is divided between A and B in the ratio 5:6. What is A's share?
questions.explanation: Total parts = 5 + 6 = 11. Value of 1 part = 462/11 = 42. A's share = 5 * 42 = 210.
questions.trick: Divide total by sum of ratios, then multiply by A's ratio digit.

questions.question: Which is the largest planet in our solar system?
Find the next number in the series: 11, 25, 39, 53, 67, ?
questions.explanation: The series follows an Arithmetic Progression with a constant difference of 14. So, 67 + (14) = 81.
questions.trick: Always check the difference between the first and second terms and verify across the series.

questions.question: If BAT is coded as DCV, how will CUP be coded in that same language?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by exactly 2 positions in the English alphabet.
questions.trick: Note the position gap between the first letter of the word and the first letter of its code.

questions.question: Who was the first President of India?
Which is the longest river in the world?
Find the odd one out among the following numbers: 101, 36, 25, 49
questions.explanation: 25, 36, and 49 are perfect squares. 101 is not a perfect square.
questions.trick: Memorize squares up to 30 to instantly spot discrepancies.

questions.question: Find the next number in the series: 3, 9, 27, 81, ?
questions.explanation: The series follows a Geometric Progression with a constant multiplier ratio of 3. So, 81 * 3 = 243.
questions.trick: If numbers scale up very quickly, look for multiplication rather than addition.

questions.question: Who wrote the national anthem of India?
